summary: This is an extremely brief post from Election Day 2004, consisting of just three words urging readers to go vote. Joel Spolsky steps entirely outside his usual software development topics to make a simple civic appeal. The post contains no technical content, argument, or analysis whatsoever. It reflects the cultural moment of the 2004 U.S. presidential election between George W. Bush and John Kerry.
key_points: Joel used his popular tech blog platform to encourage civic participation on Election Day 2004
The post demonstrates that even niche technical bloggers sometimes use their audience reach for non-technical messages
At just 3 words, it is one of the shortest posts in the Joel on Software archive
key_insight: Sometimes the most important thing a person with a platform can say has nothing to do with their area of expertise.
